current_wind.description: Strong wind from your front-right (NNE), with significant gusts.

bh.shot_strategy: Due to the strong crosswind from your right, a controlled hyzer flip or anhyzer is recommended to keep the disc from turning over. Avoid exposing the underside of the disc.

fh.shot_strategy: A forehand is the preferred throw type here. Throw with a clean release and a stable to overstable disc to fight the crosswind.

bh.shot_strategy: A slight anhyzer with an overstable disc can work well here, as the wind is pushing left, helping to counter the natural right fade.

fh.shot_strategy: The wind is pushing left, which will help your natural fade. Throw a stable to overstable disc on a hyzer angle for control.

bh.shot_strategy: Headwind demands an overstable disc. Throw with a clean hyzer to combat the wind's lift.

fh.shot_strategy: An overstable driver on a hyzer angle will handle this headwind well.

bh.shot_strategy: Utilize an overstable disc and a hyzer release to fight the strong headwind.

fh.shot_strategy: A hyzer flip with an overstable driver is your best bet against this headwind.

bh.shot_strategy: This strong headwind requires an overstable disc thrown with a hyzer to maintain control.

fh.shot_strategy: Focus on a controlled forehand with an overstable disc to overcome the headwind.

bh.shot_strategy: Throw an overstable disc on hyzer to fight the strong headwind.

fh.shot_strategy: A stable forehand with an overstable driver is recommended against this strong headwind.

bh.shot_strategy: This is a pure headwind; use your most overstable driver and a hyzer release to keep it from turning over.

fh.shot_strategy: A solid forehand with an overstable driver is your best bet. Focus on a clean, controlled throw.

bh.shot_strategy: Throw an overstable disc on a hyzer to fight this significant headwind.

fh.shot_strategy: A hyzer forehand with an overstable driver will help you manage this strong headwind.

bh.shot_strategy: Use an understable disc to take advantage of the tailwind for extra distance. Release flat or with slight hyzer.

fh.shot_strategy: An understable disc thrown with a smooth forehand will glide well in this tailwind.

bh.shot_strategy: Take advantage of the tailwind with an understable disc for maximum distance. A flat release is ideal.

fh.shot_strategy: A smooth forehand with an understable disc will get great distance in this tailwind.

bh.shot_strategy: Moderate crosswind from the right. A hyzer with a stable driver can work, but be mindful of the wind. Consider a forehand.

fh.shot_strategy: A forehand with a stable driver is a good option here. Control your angle to keep it from drifting too far right.

bh.shot_strategy: The wind is pushing left, which helps your natural fade. Throw a stable to overstable driver on a hyzer angle for control.

fh.shot_strategy: The wind will aid your natural fade. A stable forehand should get good distance and accuracy.
